title: Gharn
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{Character |type=Jedi |image=[[File:Gharn.jpg]] |name=Gharn |homeworld= |birth= |death=c. [[3964 BBY]], [[Taris/Legends|Taris]][[Knights of the Old Republic 1|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 1]] |species=[[Nagai/Legends|Nagai]]{{KOTORbackups|0}} |gender= |pronouns=He/him{{CSWECite|I|337|Gharn}} |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Black]] |feathers= |eyes= |skin=Violet |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=*[[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] **[[Taris Four]]{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/fiction/f20080409/index.html|text="Labor Pains"|int=Labor Pains|newhost=Suvudu}} |masters=[[Xamar]]''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic Handbook]]'' |apprentices= }} {{Quote|We keep our counsel. The Order does not explain—''"
"''But the Order follows your recommendations, right?|Feln and Gharn, before the Padawan Massacre|[[Knights of the Old Republic 6|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 6]]}} '''Gharn''' was a [[Nagai/Legends|Nagai]] [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] who was active in the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] during the era of the [[Mandalorian Wars]]. The Padawan of the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Xamar]], one of five stationed at the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Taris/Legends|Taris's]] [[Jedi Tower]], Gharn was close friends with four other Padawans in his class—[[Human/Legends|Humans]] [[Zayne Carrick]] and [[Shad Jelavan]], the [[Ho'Din/Legends|Ho'Din]] [[Oojoh]], and [[Kamlin]], a [[Falleen/Legends|Falleen]]. One of very few Nagai Jedi candidates, Gharn, was close to being named a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] in approximately [[3964 BBY]]. Gharn's final [[Jedi Trials/Legends|test]] as a Padawan, along with the rest of his class, was to cross Taris's [[Rogue Moon|rogue moon]], which was constantly bombarded by [[Meteor/Legends|meteors]], blind. All of the Padawans completed the trial, and a banquet and ceremony was set up shortly afterward, where the class would learn which of them would be Knighted. However, the five Jedi Masters of the Taris Jedi Tower—secretly comprising a [[Jedi Covenant]] dedicated to watching for the rise of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]]—had decided that their class of prospective Jedi would bring about the return of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]]. After the banquet, the Jedi Masters [[Padawan Massacre|slew]] Gharn and his friends. Carrick managed to escape due to a [[Time/Legends|timely]] late arrival but was officially blamed for the murders. ==Biography== ===Promising apprentice=== {{Quote|If the number of Knights on Taris were to double overnight, it wouldn't trouble me in the least.|[[Constable/Legends|Constable]] [[Noana Sowrs]], regarding the Jedi class on Taris|}} Gharn was a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Nagai/Legends|Nagai]] who was discovered and taken in by the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] as a youth.{{CSWECite|I|337|Gharn}} As a [[Youngling/Legends|youngling]], Gharn lived at the [[Jedi Enclave]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]], where[[Knights of the Old Republic 2|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 2]] he became fast friends with four of his classmates—[[Human/Legends|Humans]] [[Zayne Carrick]] and [[Shad Jelavan]], the [[Ho'Din/Legends|Ho'Din]] [[Oojoh]], and [[Kamlin]], a [[Falleen/Legends|Falleen]]. The five younglings grew up together,[[Knights of the Old Republic 4|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 4]] and at one point as younglings Gharn and Kamlin played with [[Clay/Legends|clay]] together at the Enclave. Along with his friends, Gharn eventually became a [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] at the [[Jedi Tower]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] planet [[Taris/Legends|Taris]]. Gharn [[Jedi training/Legends|trained]] directly under the [[Khil]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Xamar]], one of five [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] Masters in the academy alongside Human [[Lucien Draay]], the [[Miraluka/Legends|Miraluka]] [[Q'Anilia]], the [[Feeorin/Legends|Feeorin]] [[Feln]], and [[Raana Tey]], a [[Togruta/Legends|Togruta]]. With his friends, Gharn, a rarity as a Nagai Jedi candidate, neared [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Knighthood]] in approximately [[3964 BBY]]. [[File:Rogue_moon_trial.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Gharn and his classmates descend onto the rogue moon for their last trial.]] Around that [[Standard year/Legends|year]], Gharn gathered with his classmates for what was to be his final [[Jedi Trials/Legends|test]] as a Jedi Padawan. He and his friends would travel to Taris's [[Rogue Moon]], a [[Planetoid/Legends|planetoid]] in the [[Taris asteroid belt]] that was perpetually bombarded by thousands of [[Meteor/Legends|meteors]] an [[Standard hour/Legends|hour]], and travel blind, trusting only in [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to guide them, to their Masters' encampment a long distance away. To reach the [[Moon/Legends|moon]], the Padawans had to endure a rough [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] ride through the [[Asteroid field/Legends|asteroid belt]] before arriving high above the moon. At one point, Gharn assumed Carrick was asleep and tried to wake him up, but Carrick was just closing his [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] to avoid looking at where they were. After a quick speech from Master Draay, Gharn and the others were dropped from their shuttle, through the [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] belt, and to the moon below.[[Knights of the Old Republic 3|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 3]] Wearing an [[Environment suit/Legends|environment suit]] with his [[Blast shield/Legends|blast shield]] down, Gharn successfully traversed the treacherous terrain and passed the test. His classmates also succeeded, with Carrick arriving last. However, unbeknownst to Gharn and the other Padawans, their fate had already been decided.[[Knights of the Old Republic 5|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 5]] The five Jedi Masters of Taris actually comprised a secret [[Jedi Covenant]], dedicated to guard against the return of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]]. As they waited for the Padawans to make it to their camp, the Masters set down to [[Meditation/Legends|meditate]]—together, they shared a [[Force vision/Legends|vision]] of a figure in a [[Color/Legends|red]] environment suit, the same that the Padawans were currently wearing, [[Death/Legends|killing]] them all and driving the Jedi Order and [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] to ruin. Believing one of their apprentices to be their destroyer, the Covenant decided that they would have to kill them all in a preemptive strike. ===The Padawan Massacre=== {{Quote|It's okay, ''Shad''. As usual, you're not the last one here.|Gharn, to Shad Jelavan|}} [[File:XamarKillsGharn-KOTOR1.png|thumb|right|180px|Gharn, slain by his Master, Xamar]] After the group made it home, the Masters cloistered themselves for [[Standard day/Legends|days]]; when they emerged, they declared that they had determined who was to be promoted to Jedi Knight and set up a banquet and a ceremony, where the Padawans would learn their fates. Gharn attended the festivities with his classmates—Carrick, notoriously tardy, arrived late, crashing through a [[Window/Legends|window]] while in pursuit of a [[Marn Hierogryph|criminal]]. Afterward, the Padawans retired to the Masters' chambers to discover who would be Knighted. Shad Jelavan arrived late, but Gharn assured him he was not the last to arrive at that Carrick was still missing. While they waited for Carrick, the masters revealed that Carrick was to be knighted too, exciting Gharn and the others. However, Jelavan suspected something was amiss, as he was unwilling to believe that Carrick, by far the least of their class, would actually be made a Jedi Knight. Jelavan then tried to get the Masters to tell them what was really going on. Gharn and the others followed Jelavan's lead, until suddenly the Masters ignited their [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]] and attacked. The Padawans had no chance, and Gharn was [[Padawan Massacre|slain]] alongside Kamlin, Jelavan, and Oojoh. Carrick, who arrived late to find his friends' corpses, escaped the Masters' pursuit after a wild chase. Carrick was blamed for the murders, and he—along with an "accessory," local [[Snivvian/Legends|Snivvian]] hood Marn Hierogryph—had a sizable [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] placed on his head. Meanwhile, the bodies of Gharn and his friends were carried away by [[Medical droid/Legends|medical droids]]. The story of the massacre became a huge news focus on Taris, and [[Hologram/Legends|holograms]] of Gharn and his class could be found as far deep as the [[Lower City]]. Gharn and his friends were publicly mourned sometime afterward, with the Masters of the Covenant speaking kindly of them at the [[Funeral/Legends|funerals]].[[Knights of the Old Republic 9|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 9]] The [[3963 BBY|year after]] the murders, Raana Tey, her sanity eroding, had a nightmarish vision in which Gharn and his Jedi class took the place of the Jedi Covenant, and she took the place of Zayne Carrick, walking in on the murders of her fellow Masters. In her dream, Gharn wielded a red lightsaber, such as those that the Sith used.[[Knights of the Old Republic 16|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 16]] Xamar, Gharn's Master, would later confess to the murders to the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi Council]] and clear Carrick's name.[[Knights of the Old Republic 32|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 32]] [[Gharn's father]] eventually joined Carrick's [[Rogue Moon Project]], an organization dedicated to helping refugees, wrongly accused fugitives, and the like.[[Knights of the Old Republic 48|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 48]] ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|You sure do get the teachers' attention, my friend.''"
"''Maybe you should try getting less of it!|Shad Jelavan and Gharn, to Zayne Carrick|}} Gharn had violet skin and short dark [[Hair/Legends|hair]] with a [[Padawan braid/Legends|Padawan braid]]. He was close friends with the four other Padawans in his class, having grown up with them on Dantooine and Taris. Gharn was especially close with Zayne Carrick—although he enjoyed ribbing the Human, Gharn was overjoyed when he heard that Carrick was going to be Knighted. However, when Shad Jelavan challenged the Taris Jedi Masters on Carrick's potential Knighthood, Gharn followed Jelavan's lead, questioning whether they actually would make Carrick a Jedi Knight. ==Powers and abilities== Gharn had a very strong connection to the Force, and, by 3964 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]], he was adept enough to be nearing the rank of Jedi Knight. Gharn had the ability to [[Force sight|see]] through the Force when his sensory vision was taken away, allowing him to survive his trial on the rogue moon. ==Equipment== On Taris, Gharn wore a [[Jedi apparel/Legends|Jedi robe]], along with a [[Utility belt/Legends|belt]] with several pouches and a pair of [[Gauntlet/Legends|gauntlets]] that covered his [[Limb/Legends|hands]] and forearms. He also had his own blue lightsaber, which was kept in the Jedi Tower along with those of his friends after the Padawan Massacre.[[Knights of the Old Republic 24|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 24]] ==Behind the scenes== Gharn made his first appearance, albeit unidentified, in [[Knights of the Old Republic 1|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 1]], a [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] issue written by [[John Jackson Miller]], illustrated by [[Brian Ching]], and published by [[Dark Horse Comics]] on [[January 25]], [[2006]].{{DarkHorse|url=Comics/12-857/Star-Wars-Knights-of-the-Old-Republic-1|text=Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic #1}} He was first identified as Gharn in ''[[The Taris Holofeed: Prime Edition]]'', an [[in-universe]] news report written by Miller and published by Dark Horse in the comic issue ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic/Rebellion flip-book]]'' on [[March 1]], [[2006]].{{DarkHorse|url=Comics/13-539/Star-Wars-Knights-of-the-Old-Republic-Rebellion-Flip-Book|text=Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic/Rebellion Flip Book}} ==Appearances==
